title: cyperus sanguinolentus subsp. nairobiensis
intro: Cyperus sanguinolentus subsp. nairobiensis is a subspecies of the Cyperus sanguinolentus plant, commonly known as red papyrus, which is a species of sedge that grows in wetland areas. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments. The health benefits of Cyperus sanguinolentus subsp. nairobiensis include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which are applied topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
